data.title: SELLAS (SLS): Advancing AML Pipeline, Strengthened Balance Sheet, and Upcoming Catalysts Drive Higher Target and Buy Rating
data.description:
Analyst Jason McCarthy from Maxim Group assigned a Buy rating on SELLAS Life Sciences Group and increased the price target to $10.00 from $7.00.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jason McCarthy has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors related to SELLAS’s advancing AML pipeline and strengthening fundamentals. He highlights the initiation of the first-line trial of SLS009 in combination with venetoclax and azacitidine in unfit AML patients, aligning the program with the broader industry shift toward first-line combination regimens. He also notes that SELLAS’s CDK9 inhibitor and WT1-targeted immunotherapy position the company among emerging, mutation-agnostic approaches that are increasingly gaining attention in AML.

At the same time, McCarthy points out that SELLAS’s market value has risen sharply as investors anticipate pivotal Phase 3 REGAL data for GPS and further readouts from SLS009, reflecting growing confidence in the pipeline. The company has used this momentum to reinforce its balance sheet, amassing roughly $114M in cash, which reduces financing risk and supports ongoing trials. Incorporating the stronger cash position, lower perceived program risk, and multiple upcoming catalysts, he reduces his discount and risk assumptions, leading to a higher 12‑month price target of $10 and supporting his Buy recommendation.

McCarthy covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Aprea Therapeutics, Moleculin Biotech, and Co-Diagnostics. According to TipRanks, McCarthy has an average return of -13.7% and a 30.40% success rate on recommended stocks.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
